nativeRange.habitat: Generally found in swampy wetlands, pond margins, ditches, slow streams, and shallow lake edges across the southeastern United States (Gulf and Atlantic coastal plain — Florida, Georgia, Alabama, the Carolinas, Louisiana, Texas, and neighboring states), with disjunct populations in Cuba and Tabasco, Mexico. Grows in soft, often acidic water both submerged and emersed along seasonally flooded shorelines.
nativeRange.biotope: Marsh / pond margin / slow stream margin

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) or root tabs in inert gravel
open midground planting space for grass-like clumps
medium lighting (~30-50 PAR) for compact bright-green growth
soft to moderately hard, slightly acidic to neutral water
iron and micronutrient dosing for reddish tint under brighter light

goodWith: cold-tolerant tankmates (white cloud mountain minnows, fancy goldfish in cooler tanks)
avoidWith: boisterous diggers that uproot stem plants

summary: A grass-like creeping rush from the wetlands of the southeastern US that thrives both submerged and emersed, forming bright-green clumps in the midground and tolerating an unusually wide temperature range for a tropical-aquarium plant.
careNotes: Juncus repens is the most commonly traded rush (family Juncaceae) in the planted-aquarium hobby and is generally considered easy-to-moderate in cultivation. Sources broadly agree on its grass-like, tufted growth habit and its dual emersed/submerged habit. Submerged height varies modestly across sources: Tropica cites 10-20+ cm after two months, Aquasabi 10-30 cm, and Aquarium Breeder 10-40 cm — averaged here to roughly 10-30 cm, with most home tanks settling near the lower-to-middle end. Temperature tolerance is unusually broad for an aquarium plant because the species' native range stretches from subtropical Florida to seasonally cool Mid-Atlantic and Appalachian wetlands: optimal tropical-tank range is roughly 18-26 °C (Aquarium Breeder 20-26 °C, Flowgrow optimal 18-25 °C, Aquasabi 4-28 °C tolerance), and it has been reported to survive down to about 10 °C, which makes it one of the few popular aquascape plants compatible with unheated or coldwater setups (cool fancy-goldfish tanks, white cloud mountain minnow biotopes). pH tolerance also runs wide: Aquarium Breeder 5.5-8.0, Flowgrow and Aquasabi 5-7, averaged here to 5.5-7.5 with a preference for slightly acidic-to-neutral water. Hardness is similarly forgiving (gH ~0-18, kH ~0-14). Lighting and CO2: medium light (around 30-50 PAR) is the consistent recommendation — Tropica medium, Aquarium Breeder medium, Flowgrow medium-to-high, Aquasabi medium. Under brighter light with good micronutrient dosing the plant takes on a reddish-brown tint, while under more modest light it stays bright green. CO2 is rated 'optional': Tropica lists medium demand (6-14 mg/L), but Aquarium Breeder and Aquasabi describe CO2 as 'not required' though beneficial, and Flowgrow notes 20-40 mg/L for stronger growth. The plant is generally rated easy-to-medium care and is known to handle the emersed-to-submerged transition with some leaf melt that recovers as new submerged growth appears. Placement is primarily midground for the grass-like effect, though it works as low background in shallower tanks or as a tall foreground accent. Substrate is best nutrient-rich (aquasoil); root tabs are advised if planted in inert gravel, and very fine sand is workable but should not be packed too tightly around the rhizome. Notable: this is one of the few US-native aquatic plants in common trade and a cold-tolerant option for unheated planted tanks. Trade sourcing: Tropica supplies it as the 1-2-Grow! tissue-culture item 133FTC; specialty US retailers (Aquatic Arts, ABC Plants, Glass Aqua, BRS Fresh) carry it as well; Buce Plant's main collection page did not show it at access time, so cross-verification leans on Aquarium Breeder, Flowgrow, and Aquasabi alongside Tropica.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings of the creeping rhizome-like stems, by splitting established clumps, and by separating daughter plants thrown off on lateral runners. Trim the top of a mature shoot, replant the cutting directly into the substrate (the lower internodes will root), and let the parent plant continue throwing runners. Regular trimming encourages denser, bushier clumps and helps the plant carpet outward across the midground.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica's species detail page for Juncus repens (1-2-Grow! tissue culture, item 133FTC). The provided search URL (tropica.com/en/search?q=Juncus+repens) returned only navigation chrome; resolved here to the per-species detail page. Used for: native origin (SE North America swampy areas), submerged height (10-20+ cm after two months), light (medium, ~0.5 W/L), CO2 (medium, 6-14 mg/L), growth rate (medium, 'moderate but constant'), difficulty (medium / 'undemanding'), reddish-brown hue under intense light with good micronutrients, and stem-cutting propagation.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Designated secondary did not return a Juncus repens product page on the main collection URL — species not present in the visible catalog. Fell back to Aquarium Breeder, Flowgrow, and Aquasabi for cross-source verification per playbook step 3 (try secondaries in order).

additional.name: Aquarium Breeder
additional.notes: Confirms family Juncaceae; native to SE US Gulf/Atlantic coast (FL, GA, AL, NC, SC, LA, TX and neighbors) plus western Cuba; height 10-40 cm (4-16 in); minimum tank 10 gal (~40 L); temp 20-26 °C (68-79 °F) and tolerates ~10 °C (50 °F); pH 5.5-8.0; gH 0-18; medium light (30-50 PAR, 8-10 h photoperiod); CO2 not needed to low; moderate-to-fast growth; midground placement; nutrient-rich substrate (root tabs in inert); easy-to-medium difficulty; cuttings, lateral shoots, and runner-daughter plants for propagation; not allelopathic; emersed-to-submersed melt then recovery.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Confirms family Juncaceae; range SE/E USA, Tabasco (Mexico), Cuba; temperature tolerance 4-28 °C with optimal 18-25 °C; pH 5-7; kH 0-14 °dKH; medium-to-high light; CO2 20-40 mg/L recommended; fast growth; easy difficulty; foreground/midground/background/group placement; cuttings, splitting, and daughter-plant separation for propagation; reddish hue under strong light + micronutrients.

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Confirms common names (Creeping Rush, Lesser Creeping Rush); family Juncaceae; range SE USA, Cuba, eastern Mexico; height 10-30 cm; medium light; CO2 not required but beneficial; broad temperature tolerance 4-28 °C; pH 5-7; gH 0-30 °dGH; kH 0-14 °dKH; moderate-to-fast growth; easy difficulty; foreground/midground/background/group; cuttings, splitting, daughter plants for propagation; susceptible to green spot algae under moderate light.
